title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an array of objects as input and returns a memoized version of the array. The memoized array should only re-render when the objects themselves change, not when the array reference changes. The function should also handle edge cases where the input array is empty or null.
statement.notes: Use React's memoization feature to optimize re-renders.

examples.input: [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }, { id: 2, name: 'Jane' }, { id: 3, name: 'Bob' }]
examples.output: [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }, { id: 2, name: 'Jane' }, { id: 3, name: 'Bob' }]

hints.text: Use React's `useCallback` hook to memoize the array reference.
Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object references.
Handle edge cases where the input array is empty or null.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References
editorial.intuition: Use React's memoization feature to optimize re-renders.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a brute-force approach to compare entire arrays.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use `useCallback` and `JSON.stringify` to optimize re-renders and handle edge cases.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution ensures that the memoized array only re-renders when the objects themselves change.
editorial.dryRun: Test the optimal solution with various input arrays to verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Not using `useCallback` to memoize the array reference

testCases.input: [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }, { id: 2, name: 'Jane' }, { id: 3, name: 'Bob' }]
testCases.output: [{ id: 1, name: 'John' }, { id: 2, name: 'Jane' }, { id: 3, name: 'Bob' }]
